title: Meal-Prep Crisp Dutch Shallots Bowl
description: A rich, crisp, and flavorful Dutch dish featuring caramelized shallots and served over a nutritious base, perfect for meal prep and a sugar-free diet.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the shallots to the saucepan and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and lightly browned, about 20 minutes.
Sprinkle the sea salt and chili flakes over the shallots and stir to combine.
Transfer the shallots to a large baking dish and spread evenly.
In a small bowl, whisk together the gochujang and sesame oil.
Pour the gochujang mixture over the shallots and stir to combine.
Bake the shallots in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until they are caramelized and crispy.
While the shallots are baking, cook the brown rice and roast the chicken breast according to package instructions.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked brown rice, shredded chicken, and pickled ginger.
To assemble the bowls, place a scoop of the brown rice mixture in the bottom of each Korean brass bowl, followed by a spoonful of the crispy shallots, and finish with a sprinkle of sesame seeds.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
